**Acceptance Criteria**

Blocking failure: the prior `jit plan` / `C → P` / `[planning]` issues were mostly fixed, but the skill is now internally inconsistent about template-driven gate names.

- [.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md:112) tells the agent to read gate presets from `.jit/templates.toml` and “never hardcode these names.”
- [.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/SKILL.md:410) then hardcodes `jit gate pass <B> coverage-preview`.
- [.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/references/bracket-spine.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/references/bracket-spine.md:60), [line 74](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/references/bracket-spine.md:74), and [line 81](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/jit-breakdown/references/bracket-spine.md:81) also hardcode `coverage-preview` instead of the template’s recorded breakdown coverage gate.

This is a correctness/technical-debt issue in the migrated skill docs: a ruleset with a non-default breakdown coverage gate would be discovered correctly, then ignored by the referenced execution procedure.

**Prior Review Feedback**

The specific prior blockers in the main breakdown skill are addressed: it now describes `jit apply plan <id>`, `C → B → P`, consuming pre-created `B`, and `.jit/templates.toml` instead of `[planning]`. The remaining issue above is a newly visible inconsistency in the migrated gate-running instructions.

**Architecture And Separation Of Concerns**

No production architecture boundary violation found. Rust changes reviewed are test-only additions under existing test modules.

**Functional Paradigm**

No blocking functional-style concern. The new imperative graph traversal helpers are test code and acceptable.

**Correctness And Invariants**

No production changes appear to affect atomic writes, event logging, gate transition semantics, assignee/label validation, PID handling, or unsafe code. The blocking correctness concern is documentation workflow correctness: following the hardcoded gate command can run/check the wrong gate for a template-customized bracket.

**Safety**

No unsafe code was introduced in the reviewed diff.

**Testing And Lint**

I did not run the Rust test suite because the issue has a passed `cargo-ci` gate in the supplied issue context and local gate-run evidence for this issue. I ran `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`; it passed with label warnings only, which are allowed.

**Error Handling**

No new production fallible operations were added. Test-only `unwrap()` / `expect()` usage is acceptable.

**CLI Contract**

No production CLI behavior change was introduced by this issue’s code changes. Added tests cover `jit apply plan --json` paths, but the blocker is in docs.

**Documentation**

Blocking documentation issue as above: the active skill/reference docs still do not consistently preserve the template-declared gate preset model. `project-lead` is better here: [.claude/skills/project-lead/SKILL.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/.claude/skills/project-lead/SKILL.md:126) says to use whatever gate name the template declares.

**Jit Issue Management**

The prerequisite dependency `96fc8cd8` is marked done in the supplied context. `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed with allowed warnings.

VERDICT: FAIL
